functions.name: flashdb_set
functions.params_text: struct flashdb_handle *db, const char *key, const char *value
functions.normalized_body: for (size_t i = 0; i < db->count; ++i) { if (string_equals(db.records[i].key, key)) { db->records[i].value = value; return 0; } } if (db->count >= 16) { return -1; } db->records[db->count].key = key; db->records[db->count].value = value; db->count += 1; return 0;

functions.name: flashdb_get
functions.normalized_body: for (size_t i = 0; i < db->count; ++i) { if (string_equals(db.records[i].key, key)) { return db->records[i].value; } } return NULL;

functions.name: flashdb_delete
functions.normalized_body: for (size_t i = 0; i < db->count; ++i) { if (string_equals(db.records[i].key, key)) { for (size_t j = i; j + 1 < db->count; ++j) { db->records[j] = db->records[j + 1]; } db->count -= 1; return 0; } } return -1;

analysis.readme_excerpt:
# FlashDB Local Fallback
This local fallback source tree exists so the execution orchestrator can self-check and exercise the FlashDB migration flow during repository development.
Scope:
- `src/flashdb.c` and `src/flashdb.h` define a small key/value-store flavored FlashDB subset.
- `tests/test_flashdb.c` lists the primary semantic scenarios that the Rust migration must preserve.
Acceptance for the fallback flow:
- generate `flashDB_rust/`
- pass `cargo build`
- pass `cargo test`
- keep unsafe usage below 10%
- pass the semantic equivalence gate
